description_long: This is a vintage, bright red triplane with three stacked wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century fighter aircraft such as the Fokker Dr.I. The wings are straight and feature prominent black crosses on white backgrounds, a historical insignia. The fuselage is slender and tapers to a pointed tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers. The front of the airplane is equipped with a wooden propeller and two main landing wheels, while the rear has a small tail skid. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield visible. The overall construction appears to be primarily wood and fabric, with some metal components for structural support and landing gear.
description: A classic red triplane with three stacked wings, black cross insignias, an open cockpit, and a wooden propeller, evoking early 20th-century fighter aircraft.
description_view_0: Front view showing the triple stacked wings, black cross insignias, and the pointed nose with the propeller barely visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open cockpit, the length of the fuselage, the wooden propeller, and the tail skid.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, dual horizontal stabilizers, single vertical stabilizer, and the two main landing wheels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cockpit, wing structure, and the tail section with the cross insignia on the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, mid-mounted wings, no winglets, and no visible dihedral or anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
